steps: 5X Phusion HF or GC Buffer GC buffer should be used in experiments where HF buffer does not work. Detergent-free reaction buffers are also available for applications that do not tolerate detergents (e.g. microarray, DHPLC). 5X Phusion HF Buffer and 5X Phusion GC Buffer are provided with the enzyme. HF buffer is recommended as the default buffer for high-fidelity amplification. For difficult templates, such as GC-rich templates or those with secondary structure, GC buffer can improve reaction performance.
10 mM dNTPs Phusion cannot incorporate dUTP. Deoxynucleotide Solution Mix - 8 umol of each New England Biolabs Catalog #N0447S

DMSO (optional) It is important to note that if a high concentration of DMSO is used, the annealing temperature must be lowered as it decreases the primer Tm (2). Amplification of difficult targets, such as those with GC-rich sequences or secondary structure, may be improved by the presence of additives such as DMSO (included). A final concentration of 3% DMSO is recommended, although concentration can be optimized in 2% increments.
